name: Carmen Mastren
abstract: Carmen Mastren (born Carmine Nicholas Mastrandrea, October 6, 1913 – March 31, 1981) was an American jazz guitarist, banjoist, and violinist who was a member of the Tommy Dorsey orchestra from 1936 to 1941.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Louis Armstrong
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Jelly Roll Morton
New York Jazz Repertory Company: Seventy Five Years of Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: With the Delta Four for this recording session, there was Pete Brown, Carmen Mastren, Gene Traxler and a singer named Dell St. John.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: We had ... Carmen Mastren who played guitar with Tommy Dorsey. And they — Dill Jones ... So you know, we tried to put as many of these great musicians to work
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007
